summary: The input batch underwent a rigorous 8-protocol scholarly analysis. Several merged words were successfully split into their constituent parts, including 'ገንዘብመጠጣት' (money, to drink), 'መበሳጨትእስኪሪብቶ' (frustration, pen), 'ማረሻልኳንዳ' (plow, butcher shop), 'እንቅልፍተስፋ' (sleep, hope), 'ምርቃናእውነት' (dizziness, truth), 'ድንክውሸት' (dwarf, lie), and 'የድሃውና' (of the poor one, and). One visual OCR correction was made: 'ባስተሰሰብም' was corrected to 'ባስተሳሰብም' to reflect the correct Amharic word for 'by thought also', as 'ሰ' and 'ሳ' are common OCR confusions. All words were lemmatized to their global citation forms (infinitives or base nouns) and assigned a major Part of Speech category. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable noise, and all retained words were at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint. Scholarly guesses were primarily applied in the lemmatization of complex verbal and nominal forms, ensuring the most accurate base word was identified.

The input batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. One word, 'ግንኙነትአባዜ', was split into two distinct nouns: 'ግንኙነት' (relationship) and 'አባዜ' (obsession/bad habit), as they represent separate morphological units. A visual OCR correction was applied to 'ስተነግሩኝ', which was corrected to 'ስትነግሩኝ' to align with the verb 'መንገር' (to tell). Another scholarly guess was made for 'ተጋርጠ', which was corrected to 'ተጋርጦ' to derive its root from 'መጋረድ' (to block/obstruct), assuming a common OCR truncation or error. No words were discarded for being gibberish or single characters. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged with their major part of speech.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words containing the conjunction 'ና' (and) were split: 'ቦርኒኦና' into 'ቦርኒኦ' and 'ና'; 'አካዴሚያዊና' into 'አካዴሚያዊ' and 'ና'; 'ማስጠንቀቁንና' into 'ማስጠንቀቁ' (root 'ማስጠንቀቅ') and 'ና'; and 'ከመጋጨታቸውና' into 'ከመጋጨታቸው' (root 'መጋጨት') and 'ና'. A scholarly guess was made for 'ባያስከፋቸ', correcting the final 'ቸ' to 'ቸው' for proper lemmatization to 'ማስከፋት', as 'ቸ' is a common OCR error or informal variant for 'ቸው' in this context. All words and their derived roots met the minimum two-character length requirement. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable noise, as all provided semantic or functional value.

summary: Processed 30 input tokens. Discarded 'ኒክሬክ', 'አፎንሶ', 'በአልካሱቨሽ', 'የካነሪ' as proper nouns/foreign entities lacking clear Amharic roots or semantic utility. Corrected 'ምፍትሔ' to 'መፍትሔ' and 'ይችግሮቹ' to 'ችግሮቹ'. Split 'ስቃይበኢትዮጵያ' into 'ስቃይ' and 'ኢትዮጵያ'. 'ከአቸውም' and 'የረጅምር' were discarded as OCR noise/unintelligible fragments.

The input batch underwent rigorous processing. Several words were split to isolate their constituent parts: 'ያልተያዙበትንና' into 'ያልተያዙበትን' and 'ና'; 'ወደቤትህ' into 'ወደ' and 'ቤትህ'; 'ጓደኞቼጋ' into 'ጓደኞቼ' and 'ጋ'; 'ስተውና' into 'ስተው' and 'ና'; 'ለመፈረጅና' into 'ለመፈረጅ' and 'ና'; and 'ሊወርርና' into 'ሊወርር' and 'ና'. Two words were discarded: 'ፋጐሎና' was identified as a likely proper noun or foreign word, lacking a clear Amharic root for general lexicographical inclusion. 'ቢልቭ' was discarded as a direct transliteration of the English word 'believe', falling outside the scope of Amharic root analysis. Scholarly guesses were applied for 'ያስተናል', which was lemmatized to 'ማስተላለፍ' based on morphological patterns suggesting a common conjugation of the verb 'to transmit'. Similarly, 'ባለመጥባታቸው' was rooted to 'መጥባት', recognizing 'ባለ' as a prefix indicating negation or lack, and 'ለባለሙያተኛ' was rooted to 'ባለሙያ', interpreting 'ተኛ' as an emphatic suffix rather than a core part of the lemma.

The input batch underwent a rigorous 8-protocol scholarly analysis. Several words required splitting from their attached particles or affixes, such as 'ማረጋጋጥና' (split into 'ማረጋገጥ' and 'ና'), 'ለህብረተሱቡ' (split into 'ህብረተሰብ' and 'ለ', 'ኡ'), and 'የሚያጓትቱበትና' (split into 'ማጓተት' and 'የ', 'ኡበት', 'ና'). All discarded particles and affixes (e.g., 'ና', 'ም', 'ስ', 'ኡ', 'ው', 'የ', 'ከ', 'ለ', 'በ') were removed as they do not constitute dictionary entry forms. Visual OCR corrections were applied to words like 'ማረጋጋጥና' (corrected to 'ማረጋገጥ' for standard spelling), 'ለህብረተሱቡ' (corrected to 'ህብረተሰቡ' for 'ሱ' to 'ሰ' confusion), 'አይነግሩዋቸውም' (corrected to 'አይነግሯቸውም' for 'ዋ' to 'ሯ'), 'ይኄ' (corrected to 'ይሄ'), 'አላብዳትም' (corrected to 'አልሰጣትም' for 'ብ' to 'ሰ' confusion), 'ጉዋንግዱግ' (transliterated to 'ጓንግዶንግ'), 'የሀውልስታትን' (corrected to 'ሐውልት' for 'ሀ' to 'ሐ' and plural marker 'ታት' to 'ት'), 'በቅቱዋል' (corrected to 'በቅቷል' for 'ቱ' to 'ቷ'), 'ባለፉትት' (removed redundant 'ት'), and 'ጨዋታታዎች' (removed redundant 'ታ'). A scholarly guess was made for 'እየተሰማኘ', which was morphologically challenging; it was interpreted as a corrupted form of 'መሰናበት' (to bid farewell) based on the 'ኘ' ending and common verbal patterns. All roots and cleaned words are at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint. Each word was lemmatized to its global citation form (infinitive for verbs, base form for nouns/adjectives), assigned a major Part of Speech, and provided with comprehensive English synonyms.

The input batch underwent rigorous processing according to the 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting: 'የመጀመርያዎቹለመጀመርያ' into 'የመጀመርያዎቹ' and 'ለመጀመርያ'; 'ያለማህበራዊ' into 'ያለ' and 'ማህበራዊ'; 'የጠጣኸውና' into 'የጠጣኸው' and 'እና'; 'ሱሲንዮስና' into 'ሱሲንዮስ' and 'እና'; 'ዲቬሎፕመንትና' into 'ዲቬሎፕመንት' and 'እና'; and 'እየተንቀሳቀስንና' into 'እየተንቀሳቀስን' and 'እና'. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable noise. Visual corrections were applied to 'የኣለቃው' (corrected 'ኣ' to 'አ') and 'ናችወ' (corrected to 'ናቸው'). Scholarly guesses were made for lemmatization where direct stripping was insufficient: 'ደፉዋት' was rooted to 'መድፋት' (active voice 'to pour/spill') to capture the core action; 'መካያው' was rooted to 'መካከለኛ' (middle/mediator); 'ባለአበባ' and 'ጤናዳም' were treated as compound nouns/adjectives. All words and their derived roots met the minimum two-character length requirement.

The input batch underwent a rigorous 8-protocol scholarly analysis. Several words were split from their suffixes: 'የተጠቃና' (to 'የተጠቃ'), 'ስለሚርቀውና' (to 'ስለሚርቀው'), 'ከመተረክና' (to 'ከመተረክ'), 'ተጠያቂማ' (to 'ተጠያቂ'), 'ተጠናቅረውም' (to 'ተጠናቅረው'), 'ጠያይቀውና' (to 'ጠያይቀው'), 'ከመሰሪነትም' (to 'ከመሰሪነት'), 'በዘመዶቹም' (to 'ዘመድ'), and 'በወዳጆቹም' (to 'ወዳጅ'). Two words, 'ባዮግ' and 'በድዋ', were discarded as unfixable OCR noise or gibberish due to their ambiguity and lack of clear Amharic meaning without further context. Scholarly guesses and corrections were applied to several entries: 'የዋስል' was rooted to 'ዋስ' assuming 'ል' was noise; 'ስረተዋል' was corrected to the more common 'ሰርተዋል' (to work) and lemmatized to 'መስራት'; 'የባንከ' was corrected to 'ባንክ'; 'በተሙዋሉበት' was corrected to 'በተሞሉበት' and lemmatized to 'መሞላት'; 'ቈቈሳው' was lemmatized to 'ቈቈሳ' acknowledging its less common usage; 'በመቻለቻው' was corrected to 'መቻቻል' (tolerance); 'በበታቾቻቸው' was lemmatized to 'በታች' (subordinate); and 'በማኝኛውም' was corrected to 'ማንኛውም' (any/every). All remaining words and their derived roots are at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint. Part of Speech tags were strictly assigned from the predefined list.

The input batch underwent a rigorous 8-protocol scholarly analysis. Protocol 1 (The Splitter) was applied to separate merged words and suffixes, resulting in splits such as 'የመደጎሚያና' into 'የመደጎሚያ', 'እስከፍተኛው' into 'ከፍተኛው', and 'ካዳናም' into 'ካዳነ'. Protocol 2 (The Judge) and Protocol 7 (The Filter) led to the discarding of single-character suffixes ('ም', 'ን'), prepositions ('ከ', 'እስከ'), and the conjunction 'እና' as they do not form standalone dictionary entries or are below the minimum character length. The fragment 'ዶርን' from 'ሃሞትዶርን' was discarded as unidentifiable gibberish, retaining only 'ሃሞት'. Protocol 3 (The Corrector) addressed visual OCR confusions: 'በምትለብው' was corrected to 'በምትለብሰው', 'ፊናንስነት' to 'ፋይናንስነት', 'እንዲጋረ' to 'እንዲጋር', and 'ተእድገት' to 'ከእድገት'. Protocol 4 (The Lemmatizer) identified the global citation form for each word, ensuring the base dictionary entry was captured. Protocol 5 (The Translator) provided comprehensive English synonyms, and Protocol 6 (The Grammarian) assigned major Part of Speech categories. All words included in the final dataset adhere to the minimum two-character length requirement.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting to isolate the core semantic unit from prefixes, suffixes, or conjunctions. Specifically:
- 'እየተከናወኑና' was split into 'እየተከናወኑ' and the conjunction 'እና'. The root 'መከናወን' was extracted.
- 'በሴንቴምበር' was split into the preposition 'በ' and the noun 'ሴንቴምበር'.
- 'በጎብኚዋ' was split into the preposition 'በ' and the noun 'ጎብኚ'.
- 'ብላተርንም' was split into the proper noun 'ብላተር' and the suffix 'ም' (implying 'እና' - also/even).
- 'ለነቢያቱ' was split into the preposition 'ለ' and the noun 'ነቢይ'.
- 'ባለትልልቅ' was split into the prefix 'ባለ' (having) and the adjective 'ትልቅ'.
- 'መንዛዛትም' was split into the verb 'መንዛዛት' and the suffix 'ም' (implying 'እና' - also/even).
- 'የእስክንድሪያውም' was split into the possessive 'የ', the proper noun 'እስክንድሪያ', and the suffix 'ም' (implying 'እና' - also/even).
- 'የበሽተኞቹን' was split into the possessive 'የ' and the noun 'በሽተኛ'.
- 'እያኮረፈችና' was split into 'እያኮረፈች' and the conjunction 'እና'. The root 'ማኮርፈፍ' was extracted.
- 'የሩቁንና' was split into the possessive 'የ', the adjective 'ሩቅ', and the conjunction 'እና'.
- 'የዋትሰን' was split into the possessive 'የ' and the proper noun 'ዋትሰን'.
- 'እሱላይ' was split into the pronoun 'እሱ' and the postposition 'ላይ'.

All extracted roots are at least two characters long, adhering to Protocol 7. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable noise (Protocol 2) or for being single characters (Protocol 7). Visual repairs (Protocol 3) were applied where necessary to ensure correct Amharic script. Lemmatization (Protocol 4) focused on identifying the global citation form for each word. English synonyms (Protocol 5) were provided to capture the full semantic range. Part of Speech tagging (Protocol 6) adhered strictly to the specified major categories.
